title: CICADA SESSION
subtitle: The Booth · 9:18 PM · Late Summer
epigraph_upright: Three appointments tonight, all of them small. The cicadas are doing the part of the work the room can't do. Elicia gets to be the listener instead of the engineer for a change. The reel is fresh.
epigraph_reversed: She loses the take. The reel runs out before the writer reads the chapter. The kid leaves with the letter unread. The booth is quieter than it should be when somebody's been here.
scene_description: Three visitors on the books for the evening, low pressure, the booth windows open a crack so the cicadas can do their share of the soundtrack. Elicia at the console. Coffee on. The tape is new — a fresh master reel from the supply cabinet, half a millimetre of dust on it she wiped before threading. The night is finite, but generously so.
direction_hint: Get all three appointments through their session. Record. Transcribe. Play back. Don't let the master reel fill before the writer's chapter lands.
opening_log_lines: the cicadas are early tonight. early and loud.
the lounge has the returner in it already — twenty minutes ahead of schedule.
the master reel is fresh. you threaded it yourself.

stagnation_thresholds.label: the cicadas are louder than they should be.
the room is asking when tonight ends.
the session is over and nothing got recorded.

doubt_thresholds.label: elicia at the console, fresh tape on the reel.
she is starting to wonder if the take was clean.
she doesn't trust her own ear tonight.
the listener breaks at her own console.

notes: Easy-mode Priestess scenario. Three guaranteed easy connections plus Elicia herself coming in off-shift on turn 5 (or Frasier if Elicia is the player). The master reel is the doom clock — fills by 1 per RECORD action plus +2 from a single gravity card. Reel-full is a loss but unlikely on this scenario given the small visitor count. Win is 3 connections + leap from any threshold.
ambient_pool: the cicadas. the cicadas. the cicadas.
the kettle in the kitchenette has been almost-boiling for forty minutes.
the booth's red record-light is on. you don't remember turning it on.
the master reel turns. it sounds like it remembers the song it's about to record.
